Looking at the images, I can see the step went from 416 to 417, and the targets are still there. The SPACE shot didn't seem to destroy the target. Let me check what changed between frames to understand what happened.
The targets appear to still be in the same positions. Let me check if the target was actually destroyed or if something else happened. The board_changed was true, so something did change.
Let me inspect the current frame to see what's there now.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check what's on the board now after SPACE
nodes = current_frame.segmentation['nodes']
for n in nodes:
    if n['pixels'] >= 3:
        b = n['boundary']
        min_r = min(p[0] for p in b)
        max_r = max(p[0] for p in b)
        min_c = min(p[1] for p in b)
        max_c = max(p[1] for p in b)
        print(f"Color={n['color']}, pixels={n['pixels']}, bbox=({min_r},{min_c})-({max_r},{max_c})")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Interesting! After pressing SPACE:
- The target at (36,0)-(40,3) which was white (W) before is now gray (G) at (36,0)-(39,3) - it changed color from W to G, and there's a new white (W) strip at (40,0)-(40,3)
- The pink counter bar shrank from 60 to 59 pixels, and the charcoal section grew from 4 to 5 pixels
- So the target WAS hit but not destroyed - it changed from W (white) to G (gray)
This means targets need multiple hits! Or maybe I need to be aligned differently. Let me look more carefully:
- Before: W object at (36,0)-(40,3) with blue center
- After: G object at (36,0)-(39,3) with blue center, plus W strip at (40,0)-(40,3)
The target seems to have been "damaged" - it shrank from 5 rows to 4 rows and changed color. The W strip at the bottom might be a projectile or effect.
Let me try shooting again at the same position since the target is still there.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Target still exists but damaged (W->G, shrank). Shoot again to destroy it.
action(['SPACE'])
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
